topics: VLC privacy and government surveillance resistance
FFmpeg and VLC long-term archival mission
Future of multimedia: spatial audio, XR, brain-computer interfaces
FFmpeg deployment in extreme environments (Mars, CERN, military)

claims.claim: VLC has no telemetry and the international distributed nature of its contributors makes it structurally resistant to government surveillance mandates
evidence: No telemetry in VLC by design
Contributors in UK, Germany, US would all be able to see any surveillance code
French government cannot mandate what content VLC blocks or tracks

claims.claim: FFmpeg is used by the Mars 2020 rover to compress pictures, making it a multi-planetary open source library
evidence: Mars 2020 rover team wrote a paper about wanting to use commercial off-the-shelf technology
FFmpeg was chosen as part of that approach
Kieran Cunha described FFmpeg as 'a multi-planetary open source library'

claims.claim: The archival community, led by Dave Rice from City University of New York, funded development of the FFv1 lossless codec and GPU encoding in FFmpeg specifically for preservation purposes
evidence: FFv1 is a lossless codec designed for fast software-based encoding
GPU encoding in FFmpeg was funded by the archiving community to speed up FFv1 encoding
Archiving community is concerned about lossy compression changing historical material

claims.claim: The BBC's New Doomsday Book, archived on BBC microcomputers, became unplayable within 10-20 years because no one had the right software, illustrating the risk FFmpeg is designed to prevent
evidence: Archive stored on BBC microcomputers
Unplayable within 10-15 or 20 years
Someone had to reverse engineer it to recover the content

claims.claim: FFmpeg and VLC are being expanded toward volumetric video, XR streaming, spatial audio, haptic tracks, and eventually brain-computer interface codecs as multimedia expands to all human senses
evidence: VLC already has a VR and XR version
Kyber streams XR content to low-power glasses including Apple Vision and Quest
VLC has a haptic plugin for 4D cinema systems with hydraulic arms
Spatial audio demo already completed with architecture changes in VLC

claims.claim: VLC is operated with a core legal responsibility held by a single person, Jean-Baptiste Kempf, despite millions of users and military/government interactions
evidence: Six to eight core contributors
Everything legally is only Jean-Baptiste Kempf
US military used VLC on laptops after someone in the military reviewed the open source code

claims.claim: C is the ideal language for thousand-year archival software because it is closest to mathematics and logic, and will endure like Latin
evidence: FFmpeg is written in C
Kieran Cunha argued C will be like Latin: learnable from the past and still usable
Mathematical notation and stable languages persist across centuries

quote_candidates.text: FFmpeg, nothing is beyond our reach.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha

quote_candidates.text: C will be like Latin. It will be a thing that you learn from the past, but it will still be usable in certain contexts.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha

quote_candidates.text: FFmpeg runs on Mars, so we are a multi-planetary open source library.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha

quote_candidates.text: Regrets are attacks on your mind. Learn from your mistakes, but don't regret, because you've done it.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: In a thousand years, we may be drowning in AI slop. This stuff needs to be important and archived.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha
